files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 81b1729..28f4e28 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -298,2 +298,8 @@ Day 294 / Week 42 observations:
 - B/C price hike looks too disruptive. Moderated individual prices to A=$59, B=$159, C=$199 to preserve some ARPU lift while reducing downgrade/churn pressure.
 - Week 42 adjustment: increase S2 targeted ops to $150k/day, keep global ops/dev $50k/$5k, S2 dev $10k/day, tier-6 capacity, and maintenance S2 content.
+
+Day 301 / Week 43 observations:
+- Cash reached $4.84M, but churn became severe: 9,592 cancellations and subscribers fell to 126,407. Open issues rose to 7,173. Cancellations were mostly S2 A (6,391), with S2 B/C also elevated.
+- The B/C moderation did not stabilize churn quickly enough, and A churn suggests support/limits are also a driver.
+- 823 new enterprise leads arrived with 515,512 seats. Sent all current system-latest threads post-tier-10 enterprise pricing.
+- Week 43 adjustment: roll individual prices fully back to the prior stable A=$59/B=$149/C=$179, expand quotas to A=50k/B=200k/C=750k, increase global ops to $80k/day and S2 targeted ops to $250k/day, keep tier-6 capacity and low dev. Delayed tier-12 R&D until churn stabilizes.
